rationale: X directly reproduces the substance of the sole ground-truth inline comment by verifying that both submit_initial and submit_update reject list prompts, correctly explaining why the already_submitted gate was safe to drop — matching the human reviewer's actual reasoning. Y instead frames already_submitted as possible dead code to remove (self-contradicting its own earlier note that it's used elsewhere), missing the real insight, and pads its review with non-findings ('not performed due to budget') plus a fabricated issue reference (#4839) not present in any provided context, hurting precision. Both are reasonably actionable with file/line pointers, but X's claims are all grounded and verified while Y mixes valid observations with speculative/unverified ones.
